name: Lima Bean and Ham Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the lima beans by removing any debris from the package and rinsing well. Cover with about 8 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer for 2 minutes. Remove from heat, keep covered, and allow to stand for 1 hour or more.
2. While the beans are soaking, prepare the ham hocks (or use meaty ham bones). Combine the 3 cups broth and 3 cups water in a large heavy pot with lid; bring to a boil. Add the ham and simmer for about 1 hour.
3. Drain the beans, briefly rinsing them with hot water. Add the beans to the ham mixture. Slice the onions into rings and add along with the carrots, celery, and bay leaf to the pot. Mince the garlic cloves or use a garlic press; add to the mixture. Add fresh ground black pepper and salt if desired (the ham is already seasoned). Pour additional broth or hot water over this mixture until it reaches about 1 inch above the ingredients.
4. Simmer for about 1 hour, stirring several times and checking for more liquid as needed. Once the beans and vegetables are tender, remove the ham hocks or bones. Remove the meat from the bones and add back to the bean mixture. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt or pepper, adding hot pepper sauce to taste.
